reason: The younger k6s bead contains the complete consumer and epistemic-safety design. lv8.1 names only the same entity/relation query units and descriptor requirements, with no independent implementation or proof surface.
9wx's notes explicitly say the original failure is fixed and that the only remaining risk belongs to the proof-substrate seal. Current flake.nix serializes checkout SQLx bootstrap under flock.

Repository conventions and the archive README prohibit resurrecting the conductor substrate and identify pya/hlv as consumers of the same historical corpus. hlv is the richer, current authority-preserving bead.

pi1 and ej9 target the same in-repository README, concepts, positioning, vocabulary, and proof narrative. pi1 itself warns not to create a second public narrative; preserving two execution Beads would create exactly that split.

Both beads require one declaration per source, validation before an ephemeral source is admitted, and operator-visible recovery authority. r6d.8 already owns the full replayability, raw-store, cursor, loss-authority, and accepted-gap contract.
The two beads diagnose the same test, constant, fatal error path, and missing recovery behavior. r6d.13 contributes one precise control case, while 5smc preserves the design decision and archaeology.
The active bead has neither a wave nor an area label. These labels place it in the closest existing execution lane without changing priority or introducing a hard dependency.

The implementation is present, but the Bead still reads like an unlanded refactor. It is test/devloop work, so this proposal explicitly does not close it on source inspection alone.
The Bead notes verify slice E landed. Updating the body prevents a future agent from repeating the prime removal and preserves only the current residual capability work.
Accepted after graph lint confirmed the live issue violated the mandatory acceptance-criteria invariant; proposed AC is concrete and source-anchored.
The existing design says “PREREQ: none” while the graph and AC intentionally require the privacy shadow audit. This is internal specification drift, not an edge change.

h3g notes prove a safe mutable-row settling fix must change the shared SQLite cursor/checkpoint frontier and explicitly recommend sequencing after r6d.4; a source-local filter creates a skip-forever bug.

The AC requires a re-observed grown occurrence to supersede the prior reading exactly once. That behavior is the unresolved SupersedeOnChange admission contract, not implementable honestly as an h3g-local duplicate rule.

08z acceptance requires a superseded-revision outcome and exact run accounting. That outcome is defined by the still-open SupersedeOnChange admission contract; implementing a local substitute would split occurrence-revision semantics.

The entity-enricher fix is one consumer of the repository-wide civil-time policy. Landing a private timezone configuration in the enricher first would create the second time policy that 2ged exists to prevent.
replaced existing related edge with requested blocks edge

The outage demo needs any controllable source process and permission fault. Completion of the specific ActivityWatch/Hyprland data-quality cleanup is not a hard prerequisite; jdp and u3n retain the coverage/remediation gates.

Moment-search is a downstream cross-source product surface. The Phase-B authority cutover/rebuild contract depends on byte-backed observations, stable identity, lifecycle, assertions/context, and parity—not on the moment-search UI/query feature.
The surviving confirmation-durability decision is part of the substrate-honesty program and is currently structurally orphaned. This organizational edge does not alter readiness.
